name: Jerry Goldsmith
abstract: Jerrald King Goldsmith (February 10, 1929 – July 21, 2004) was an American composer, conductor and orchestrator with a career in film and television scoring that spanned nearly 50 years and over 200 productions, between 1954 and 2003. He was considered one of film music's most innovative and influential composers.

about_quotes.speaker: Howie Shear
about_quotes.evidence: Jerry Goldsmith, who passed away a few years ago, who studied with Bernard Herrmann, he’s one of my favorite scorers.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howie Shear interviewed by Monk Rowe, Dunkirk, New York, October 11, 2014

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Jerry Goldsmith was very friendly. I would go to his sessions and he would say, ' Come into the booth.' ... He would show me his scores and what not. I thought that was very nice of him.
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: Jerry Goldsmith, Hank Mancini for sure, Dave Grusin great, you know there’s some good writers out and again we are standing on the shoulders of giants.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Shorter
about_quotes.evidence: Jerry Goldsmith, I think, and some other. But that’s beyond, I would say, the front-office mentality or...
